page: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16 vector extensions, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance evaluations show top-tier single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded compression tasks placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average range. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached and uncached operations also register in the middle 50%, though PassMark memory scores remain strong.

Given the balanced resource density and strong single-core performance, this instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and text or image processing. It is also capable of handling light machine learning tasks, such as text generation with smaller language models, but lacks the local storage and GPU acceleration required for large-scale database operations or heavy deep learning training.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on network-attached storage and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and image processing scores in the top 10%, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth fall into the average range. This hardware profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for general-purpose compute tasks by offering high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU cores without the added cost of bundled local storage. It is suited for workloads such as static web serving, text and image processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for web serving and image processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.4xlarge features 16 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and top-tier single-core performance for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and image processing benchmark performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage bundled
Suited for static web serving, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference
